cards.title: Trump Administration Cracks Down on Anthropic
cards.body: The Trump administration has taken action against Anthropic, prompting discussion about what triggered these moves. The implications for the broader AI ecosystem remain uncertain.

cards.title: iOS 27 Brings Practical Apple Intelligence Features
cards.body: Apple's iOS 27 introduces multiple AI features beyond the Siri overhaul announced at WWDC. These new capabilities focus on practical utility across the operating system.

cards.title: Signal's Whittaker: AI Chatbots Aren't Friends
cards.body: Meredith Whittaker emphasizes that AI chatbots are not conscious beings or sentient interlocutors. She warns against treating them as genuine relationships.

cards.title: Nobel Laureate Jumper Leaves DeepMind for Anthropic
cards.body: John Jumper, a Nobel laureate, is departing Google DeepMind to join rival Anthropic. This marks another high-profile departure from DeepMind.

cards.title: US Government Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its newest models Fable 5 and Mythos 5 citing national security concerns. Amazon researchers reportedly discovered guardrail bypass methods.

cards.title: Anthropic Ban May Actually Boost Brand Recognition
cards.body: The forced withdrawal of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 has sparked debate about unintended marketing benefits. The controversy has increased public awareness of Anthropic's capabilities.

cards.title: Reliance Integrates AI Into 500M+ User Services
cards.body: Billionaire Ambani's Reliance is embedding AI across telecom services reaching over 500 million people. The push aims to enhance calling, apps, and home devices.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires Deductive AI for $85M
cards.body: Elastic has agreed to acquire Deductive AI, a startup using AI to catch and resolve software bugs. The deal values the three-year-old company at up to $85 million.

cards.title: AI Inference Startup Baseten Raises $1.5B
cards.body: Baseten is reportedly closing a $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation. The raise reflects ongoing investor enthusiasm for AI inference infrastructure.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Team as Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat's parent company is spinning off its AI video team into a new company called Dotmo. The move comes as Snap seeks to manage AI development costs.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Key Talent Ahead of IPO
cards.body: OpenAI has recruited Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball. Both hires signal OpenAI's IPO preparation.

cards.title: Nearly Half of US Singles Reject AI Dating
cards.body: About 47% of singles view AI negatively in dating contexts, according to Match. However, many users remain open to AI for profile enhancement and conversation assistance.

cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia With AI Chip Sales
cards.body: AWS is negotiating to sell its AI chips to other data centers, opening a potential $50 billion opportunity. CEO Andy Jassy says this represents a direct challenge to Nvidia.

cards.title: FERC Fast-Tracks Data Center Grid Access
cards.body: Federal regulators mandated that grid operators give data centers expedited interconnection approval. The move addresses infrastructure bottlenecks but doesn't solve electricity supply shortages.

cards.title: How to Disable AI in Google Docs
cards.body: Google Docs users bothered by AI writing suggestions can now disable the feature. The guide explains how to remove unwanted 'write with Gemini' prompts.

cards.title: Beyond Siri: Practical AI Features Coming to iOS 27
cards.body: Apple's iOS 27 brings useful AI features beyond Siri's overhaul announced at WWDC. The company is rolling out practical AI capabilities across the operating system.

cards.title: Signal's Whittaker: AI Chatbots Aren't Your Friends
cards.body: Meredith Whittaker warns that AI chatbots lack consciousness and sentience despite their conversational abilities. She emphasizes these tools should not be viewed as conscious beings or friends.

cards.title: Nobel Laureate Jumper Leaves DeepMind for Anthropic
cards.body: John Jumper, a Nobel laureate, is departing Google DeepMind to join rival AI company Anthropic. He's among several prominent researchers leaving Google's AI division.

cards.title: US Government Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its newest AI models citing national security concerns. Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5's safety guardrails.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires Deductive AI for Up to $85M
cards.body: Deductive AI, a startup using artificial intelligence to catch and resolve software bugs, was founded just three years ago. Elastic is expanding its AI capabilities through the acquisition.

cards.title: Baseten AI Inference Startup Raising $1.5B
cards.body: AI inference startup Baseten is reportedly finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion valuation. The funding reflects the ongoing 'inference gold rush' in the AI market.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Team into Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat is spinning off its AI video development team into a new company called Dotmo due to costs. Current Snap staff will leave to focus on AI video innovation.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Transformer Co-Inventor Before IPO
cards.body: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO by landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind. The company also hired former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball.

cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia by Selling AI Chips
cards.body: AWS is in talks to sell its AI chips to other data centers as it challenges Nvidia's dominance. CEO Andy Jassy estimates this represents a $50 billion opportunity.

cards.title: How to Turn Off AI in Google Docs
cards.body: Google's 'write with Gemini' AI feature can be disabled in Docs if users find it intrusive. The guide shows steps to remove pesky AI pop-ups from the application.

cards.title: Subquadratic Claims LLM Bottleneck Breakthrough
cards.body: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic claims it solved a mathematical bottleneck holding back large language models for nearly a decade. The startup emerged from stealth with the announcement despite initial skepticism.
